item.text: The first targeted test command was malformed because Cargo accepts only one test name filter before harness args. I’ll run the full `error` integration test after the code change, which covers all four reproduction cases.

The initial local edit used the hidden category constructors directly. I’m validating whether that preserves the `std::io::Error` message or follows the existing public builder path, then I’ll adjust if needed before tests.

The hidden internal constructor intentionally keeps the existing generic internal detail, while the serde path carries the parser message through `InvalidArgument::format`. I’m formatting and then running the package build/test loop.
Formatting completed without output. Next I’m running the changed crate’s build and integration tests from the package boundary.

item.text: The broader crate suite is green. I’m doing a final diff/status check so the submitted artifact names only the file I changed and calls out the pre-existing dirty files accurately if needed.
Only `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs` changed from my edit. The test file and `Cargo.lock` were already dirty when I started, and I left them as-is.

arguments.summary_title: Add CanonicalError conversions for io and serde_json errors
arguments.summary_markdown:
Updated `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs` with narrow `From<std::io::Error>` and `From<serde_json::Error>` implementations for `CanonicalError`.

- `std::io::Error` now converts to the existing Internal canonical error category via `Internal::new(err.to_string())`, producing status 500/title `Internal`.
- `serde_json::Error` now converts through `InvalidArgument::format(err.to_string())`, preserving the serde error message as the canonical detail and producing status 400/title `Invalid Argument`.

Verification run:
- `cargo check -p cf-modkit-canonical-errors`
- `cargo test -p cf-modkit-canonical-errors --test error`
- `cargo test -p cf-modkit-canonical-errors`
arguments.notes: The worktree already had dirty `Cargo.lock` and `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/tests/error.rs` before this edit; I did not modify them.
